data.raceComments: {"comment":"2. SUPREMELY WEST left a lesser effort behind when scoring in style over 4800m at Cheltenham last time. That performance gives him a major chance for going back to back. 17. HOLD THE SERVE continued his excellent rise through the ranks producing a third straight hurdle victory at Kempton tackling 4200m. Overall record is highly impressive and he rates among the leading hopes again. 15. FORTUNE TIMMY was outclassed among G1 company at Cheltenham. This drop in grade is significant and he cannot be dismissed outright. 9. ABSOLUTELY DOYEN won at Musselburgh two starts ago before a fair Cheltenham eighth contesting 4800m. Capable of being thereabouts.","runners":[2,17,15,9],"exclusive":false}
